---
title: "Appendix: Full cov{ŵ} derivation"
subtitle: "INFO 521 · Module 3 — supplement"
author: "Greg Chism"
institute: "School of Information · University of Arizona"
date: last-modified
date-format: "MMMM YYYY"
---

## Where we are {.smaller}

From the main deck: $\hat{\mathbf{w}}$ is unbiased ($\mathbb{E}[\hat{\mathbf{w}}] = \mathbf{w}$),
and the parameter covariance is

$$
\mathrm{cov}\{\hat{\mathbf{w}}\} \;=\; \sigma^2\left(\mathbf{X}^{\!\top}\mathbf{X}\right)^{-1}.
$$

This appendix derives that result in full. Only $\mathbf{y}$ is random;
$\mathbf{X}$ and $\mathbf{w}$ are fixed, and $\mathbb{E}[\mathbf{y}] = \mathbf{X}\mathbf{w}$,
$\;\mathrm{cov}\{\mathbf{y}\} = \sigma^2\mathbf{I}$.

::: {.notes}
Speaker note: this is the slide-10 promise paid off. Each step reveals on a
fragment — walk it line by line; the whole thing collapses through
$(\mathbf{X}^{\!\top}\mathbf{X})^{-1}\mathbf{X}^{\!\top}\mathbf{X} = \mathbf{I}$.
:::

## Set up the second moment {.smaller}

Start from the covariance definition, using $\mathbb{E}[\hat{\mathbf{w}}] = \mathbf{w}$:

$$
\mathrm{cov}\{\hat{\mathbf{w}}\} \;=\; \mathbb{E}\!\left[\hat{\mathbf{w}}\hat{\mathbf{w}}^{\!\top}\right] - \mathbf{w}\mathbf{w}^{\!\top}
$$

::: {.fragment}
Substitute $\hat{\mathbf{w}} = (\mathbf{X}^{\!\top}\mathbf{X})^{-1}\mathbf{X}^{\!\top}\mathbf{y}$
and pull the constants out of the expectation:

$$
\mathbb{E}\!\left[\hat{\mathbf{w}}\hat{\mathbf{w}}^{\!\top}\right]
= \left(\mathbf{X}^{\!\top}\mathbf{X}\right)^{-1}\mathbf{X}^{\!\top}\,
  \mathbb{E}\!\left[\mathbf{y}\mathbf{y}^{\!\top}\right]\,
  \mathbf{X}\left(\mathbf{X}^{\!\top}\mathbf{X}\right)^{-1}
$$
:::

::: {.fragment}
The data second moment, from $\mathrm{cov}\{\mathbf{y}\} = \mathbb{E}[\mathbf{y}\mathbf{y}^{\!\top}] - \mathbb{E}[\mathbf{y}]\mathbb{E}[\mathbf{y}]^{\!\top}$:

$$
\mathbb{E}\!\left[\mathbf{y}\mathbf{y}^{\!\top}\right]
= \mathbf{X}\mathbf{w}\mathbf{w}^{\!\top}\mathbf{X}^{\!\top} + \sigma^2\mathbf{I}
$$
:::

## Collect the terms {.smaller}

Substitute and use $(\mathbf{X}^{\!\top}\mathbf{X})^{-1}\mathbf{X}^{\!\top}\mathbf{X} = \mathbf{I}$ on each piece:

$$
\mathbb{E}\!\left[\hat{\mathbf{w}}\hat{\mathbf{w}}^{\!\top}\right]
= \mathbf{w}\mathbf{w}^{\!\top} + \sigma^2\left(\mathbf{X}^{\!\top}\mathbf{X}\right)^{-1}
$$

::: {.fragment}
The $\mathbf{w}\mathbf{w}^{\!\top}$ cancels against the $-\mathbf{w}\mathbf{w}^{\!\top}$ from the definition:

$$
\boxed{\;\mathrm{cov}\{\hat{\mathbf{w}}\} \;=\; \sigma^2\left(\mathbf{X}^{\!\top}\mathbf{X}\right)^{-1}\;}
$$
:::

::: {.fragment}
[And this is exactly the inverse Fisher information: from
$-\dfrac{\partial^2 \ell}{\partial\mathbf{w}\,\partial\mathbf{w}^{\!\top}} = \tfrac{1}{\sigma^2}\mathbf{X}^{\!\top}\mathbf{X} = \mathcal{I}$
(with $\ell$ the log-likelihood from the main deck),
we get $\mathrm{cov}\{\hat{\mathbf{w}}\} = \mathcal{I}^{-1}$.]{style="color: var(--accent)"}
:::

::: {.notes}
Speaker note: the cancellation is the satisfying step — the bias-free part
($\mathbf{w}\mathbf{w}^{\!\top}$) drops out, leaving pure noise scaled by the
inverse Gram matrix. Close by tying back to Fisher info (main deck slide 11).
:::
